claim_text: In nine surveyed municipal WWTP final effluents PFOA PFOS PFBS and PFHxS had 100% detection frequency and HFPO-DA had 67%; activated-sludge treatment was associated with increases of short-chain PFCAs consistent with precursor transformation
source_title: Occurrence Fate and Removal of PFAS in Small- and Large-Scale Municipal Wastewater Treatment Facilities in the United States
notes: Do not generalize detection frequencies to all plants

claim_text: Molecular simulation indicates that Arg122 substitution enlarges the hL-FABP outer pocket and disrupts PFOA hydrophobic packing and hydrogen bonding
source_title: Interaction of perfluoroalkyl acids with human liver fatty acid-binding protein
notes: Not a mutational binding experiment and does not prove a transferable dynamic material mechanism

claim_text: A cross-linked beta-cyclodextrin adsorbent removed PFAS and pharmaceuticals from municipal wastewater; affinity followed target hydrophobicity
source_title: Removal of PFAS and pharmaceuticals from municipal wastewater using a novel beta-cyclodextrin adsorbent over distinct contact times
notes: Shows a crowded high-performance host-guest baseline

claim_text: Chitosan-based molecularly imprinted polymers selectively adsorbed PFOS over structurally different anions
source_title: Selective removal of perfluorooctane sulfonate from aqueous solution using chitosan-based molecularly imprinted polymer adsorbents
notes: Demonstrates that static PFOS imprinting is not novel

claim_text: A Swiss Glatt Valley study reported 57-330 ng/L clarithromycin in treated WWTP effluent during summer
source_title: Occurrence and Fate of Macrolide Antibiotics in Wastewater Treatment Plants and in the Glatt Valley Watershed Switzerland
notes: Do not relabel the reported samples as secondary effluent without checking the process endpoint

claim_text: Macrolide antibiotics occupy defined sites in the bacterial large-ribosomal-subunit exit tunnel
source_title: The Structures of Four Macrolide Antibiotics Bound to the Large Ribosomal Subunit
notes: Supports static geometry; not sufficient for a material closing mechanism

claim_text: O-desmethylvenlafaxine showed low biodegradation and limited removal in a microalgae-based secondary-treatment system
source_title: Fate of priority pharmaceuticals and their main metabolites and transformation products in microalgae-based wastewater treatment systems
notes: Occurrence in the target local effluent remains to be measured

claim_text: SERT mediates Na+/Cl- dependent serotonin reuptake; the 2016 structures show an outward-open state and a central ligand site between TM1 3 6 8 and 10
source_title: X-ray structures and mechanism of the human serotonin transporter
notes: This paper alone does not resolve the complete alternating-access cycle and does not establish ODV-specific binding

claim_text: SMX SPY and their acetylated metabolites had the highest detection frequencies and concentrations in a 22-WWTP survey; the risk assessment identified SMX as HQ above 10 for algae
source_title: Ecotoxicity evaluation and removal of sulfonamides and their acetylated metabolites during conventional wastewater treatment
notes: Do not transfer the SMX ecological-risk result to AcSMX

claim_text: DHPS structures explain PABA/sulfonamide recognition and resistance mechanisms
source_title: Catalysis and Sulfa Drug Resistance in Dihydropteroate Synthase
notes: A reversible loop-closing material remains a design hypothesis

claim_text: ERR-gamma recognizes BPA through defined receptor interactions
source_title: Receptor binding characteristics of the endocrine disruptor bisphenol A for the human nuclear estrogen-related receptor gamma
notes: Does not independently establish a robust dynamic analogue

claim_text: In one study a carbamazepine molecularly imprinted polymer made with 4-vinylbenzoic acid reached 28.40 mg/g capacity and an imprinting factor of 2.72
source_title: Removal of the environmental pollutant carbamazepine using molecular imprinted adsorbents
notes: Evidence that conventional imprinting is crowded; not a class-wide capacity

claim_text: Nitrogen and phosphorus remain central targets for advanced treatment of wastewater secondary effluent
source_title: Simultaneous biological removal of nitrogen and phosphorus from secondary effluent of wastewater treatment plants by advanced treatment
notes: Background source

claim_text: A measured secondary effluent contained sulfate DOM nitrate and phosphorus at concentrations that create realistic competition
source_title: A comprehensive assessment on commercially available standard anion resins for tertiary treatment of municipal wastewater
notes: Use as a representative matrix not a universal composition

claim_text: Phosphate-bound C. perfringens PBP-1 is closed and completely buries phosphate between two domains using 14 hydrogen bonds from nine residues
source_title: Crystal structure of the phosphate-binding protein PBP-1 of an ABC-type phosphate transporter from Clostridium perfringens
notes: Use the exact count only for this protein

claim_text: PstS/PBP selectivity depends on protein and species; reports include at least roughly 500-fold discrimination against arsenate and up to about 10^5-scale discrimination against sulfate
source_title: Phosphate-binding proteins and peptides from molecular mechanisms to potential applications
notes: Do not present the factors as universal constants; pair quantitative claims with the relevant original study

claim_text: Nitrate-bound NrtA is a two-domain C-clamp that completely buries nitrate in the interdomain cleft; the authors propose that a C-terminal element may support transport-related conformational change
source_title: Atomic structure of a nitrate-binding protein crucial for photosynthetic productivity
notes: No paired apo/open and bound/closed structures; nitrate versus nitrite selectivity is limited

claim_text: In a model peptide covering the AfArsR metal-binding region As(III) induces a random-coil to ordered transition and forms a trigonal-pyramidal AsS3 site resembling the native protein
source_title: AsIII Selectively Induces a Disorder-to-Order Transition in the Metalloid Binding Region of the AfArsR Protein
notes: Not a whole-protein conformational experiment; municipal secondary-effluent relevance is conditional

claim_text: L-cysteine immobilized in electrospun membranes has already been presented as a bio-inspired arsenic adsorbent
source_title: Bio-inspired membranes for adsorption of arsenic via immobilized L-Cysteine in highly hydrophilic electrospun nanofibers
notes: Simple thiol immobilization is not a novel ArsR mimic

claim_text: Molecularly imprinted polymers are a mature route for pharmaceutical remediation but face real-water matrix challenges
source_title: Remediation of pharmaceuticals from contaminated water by molecularly imprinted polymers
notes: Used only to map prior-art density

claim_text: RCSB entry 1OIB is the Escherichia coli phosphate-binding protein T141D mutant and records a 321-aa protein entity
source_title: RCSB PDB 1OIB
notes: Database access verified 2026-07-16; not evidence that the project A197C construct has been expressed

claim_text: RCSB FASTA records for 1OIB and 1IXG are identical 321-aa mature chains
source_title: RCSB PDB FASTA 1OIB and 1IXG
notes: Source-chain SHA-256 78558e51049eec070b0f3249ffa8617b37a45b912436af1274ed746b57931a96; project mutations remain design definitions

claim_text: The ERRgamma-BPA complex and structure-function experiments support a preorganized two-ended polar and hydrophobic BPA-binding environment
source_title: Structural evidence for endocrine disruptor bisphenol A binding to human nuclear receptor ERR gamma
notes: Supports static geometry; does not prove BPA-induced material closure

claim_text: A beta-cyclodextrin and methacrylic-acid hybrid molecularly imprinted polymer was prepared for selective BPA adsorption from aqueous media
source_title: Removal of bisphenol A from aqueous media using a highly selective adsorbent of hybridization cyclodextrin with magnetic molecularly imprinted polymer
notes: Magnetic and imprinted composite precedent; not proof of secondary-effluent selectivity or low inactive mass

claim_text: Serum-albumin experiments show measurable PFBS binding and species- and chain-length-dependent differences between C4 and C8 PFSAs
source_title: Quantitative cross-species comparison of serum albumin binding of per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances from five structural classes
notes: Discovery signal for headgroup and finite low-polar recognition; not a PFBS-specific residue map

claim_text: A tunable cationic porous beta-cyclodextrin polymer removed multiple anionic PFASs at low loading; short-chain capture relied mainly on electrostatics and weakened in a salt matrix
source_title: A Tunable Porous beta-Cyclodextrin Polymer Platform to Understand and Improve Anionic PFAS Removal
notes: Direct platform precedent; salt and wastewater matrix remain critical for PFBS

claim_text: Ionic fluorogel resins removed a chemically diverse PFAS mixture from water including a real river-water matrix
source_title: Ionic Fluorogels for Remediation of Per- and Polyfluorinated Alkyl Substances from Water
notes: Broad PFAS capture is not PFBS selectivity and fluorinated-monomer lifecycle must be considered

claim_text: GAC and anion-exchange resin remove PFBS and PFOS through differing combinations of hydrophobic adsorption and ion exchange
source_title: Removal of Perfluorooctane and Perfluorobutane Sulfonate from Water via Carbon Adsorption and Ion Exchange
notes: Conventional engineering baseline; not a biomimetic or PFBS-selective mechanism

claim_text: A ROX-imprinted PMAA layer on silica was manufacturable but reported only a 1.21 ROX-to-erythromycin separation factor
source_title: Preparation of roxithromycin molecularly imprinted layer-coated silica and its selective adsorption properties
notes: Direct precedent also demonstrates limited selectivity and heavy-carrier penalty

claim_text: A DES-mediated COF surface-imprinted ROX material reported 64.58 mg/g capacity an imprinting factor of 2.40 and 91.4 percent capacity retention after four cycles
source_title: Eco-friendly deep eutectic solvent-mediated COF-based surface molecularly imprinted polymers for specific adsorption and determination of roxithromycin
notes: Recent single study; not yet proof of municipal secondary-effluent competitive performance

claim_text: Roxithromycin erythromycin and clarithromycin compete at the bacterial ribosomal complex and show measurable slow-binding behavior
source_title: Erythromycin Roxithromycin and Clarithromycin Use of Slow-Binding Kinetics to Compare Their In Vitro Interaction with a Bacterial Ribosomal Complex Active in Peptide Bond Formation
notes: Supports static macrolide-site occupation and kinetics; not a ROX-specific movable gate

claim_text: A polyaniline-silica surface-imprinted composite selectively adsorbed 2,6-DCP over related phenols and identified film diffusion as a major rate limitation
source_title: Selective adsorption of 2,6-dichlorophenol by surface imprinted polymers using polyaniline/silica gel composites as functional support
notes: Direct imprinting precedent; heavy silica and secondary-effluent relevance remain unresolved

claim_text: RCSB 2E2R is a 1.60-Angstrom ERRgamma-BPA structure; coordinate analysis gives a 9.274-Angstrom BPA phenolic O-O distance and an approximately 87.4-degree acute inter-ring plane angle
source_title: RCSB PDB 2E2R and Structural evidence for endocrine disruptor bisphenol A binding to human nuclear receptor ERR gamma
journal_or_authority: RCSB PDB and Journal of Biochemistry
notes: Distances and angle were recalculated from official coordinates on 2026-07-17; they define a design target and do not prove an artificial receptor

claim_text: 1,2-Di(pyridin-4-yl)ethyne is a rigid linear C12H8N2 scaffold with terminal pyridine sites; a primary study reports synthesis in 75 percent isolated yield and single-crystal characterization
source_title: Structural diversity in the solid-state architectures of bis(4-pyridyl)acetylene and its derivatives
locator: full text methods and crystal structure
notes: 2-di(pyridin-4-yl)ethyne
column14: Supports exact structure and synthetic accessibility only; no BPA binding or aqueous solubility was reported

claim_text: Spectroscopic work on BPA-imprinted systems supports hydrogen-bond interaction between BPA and 4-vinylpyridine and discusses rebinding in water
source_title: Insight into BPA-4-vinylpyridine interactions in molecularly imprinted polymers using complementary spectroscopy techniques
notes: Supports pyridine as an interaction lead; imprinting and a polymer microenvironment do not prove soluble BPE bidentate binding

claim_text: 1,3-Bis(4-pyridyl)benzene was prepared by Suzuki coupling as a rigid bis-pyridyl ligand
source_title: Coordination-Driven Self-Assembly of Silver(I) and Gold(I) Rings: Synthesis Characterization and Photophysical Studies
notes: 3-bis(4-pyridyl)benzene
column14: Supports scaffold accessibility only; endpoint matching water solubility and BPA binding remain unverified

claim_text: N-(4-pyridyl)isonicotinamide is a reported nonpolymeric hydrogelator in water at a minimum gelator concentration of 0.37 weight percent
source_title: Nonpolymeric Hydrogelator Derived from N-(4-Pyridyl)isonicotinamide
notes: Water compatibility is confounded by self-assembly; flexible amide scaffold is rejected as the primary BPA geometry probe

claim_text: 1,2-Bis(2-pyridylethynyl)benzene is a preorganized trans-chelating ligand with inward-facing pyridine nitrogens modeled about 4.3 Angstrom apart and crystallographically shown to bind Ag(I) and Pd(II) in its cavity
source_title: 1,2-Bis(2-pyridylethynyl)benzene a Novel Trans-Chelating Bipyridyl Ligand
locator: abstract crystal structures and follow-up distance report
notes: 2-bis(2-pyridylethynyl)benzene
column14: Proves a low-mass convergent topology; does not prove BPA fit aqueous binding or adsorbent performance

claim_text: ERRgamma mutagenesis identifies Glu275 and Arg316 interactions with the same BPA phenol hydroxyl as essential while a single-hydroxyl analog 4-alpha-cumylphenol still binds strongly
source_title: Receptor binding characteristics of bisphenol A for human ERRgamma; ERRgamma tethers strongly bisphenol A and 4-alpha-cumylphenol
journal_or_authority: FEBS Journal and Biochemical and Biophysical Research Communications
locator: article abstracts and reported binding interpretation
notes: Supports asymmetric primary-anchor translation; does not prove a particular artificial receptor

claim_text: 2-Methacrylamidopyridine was prepared from 2-aminopyridine and methacryloyl chloride and was free-radically polymerized with AIBN in acetonitrile
source_title: Characterization and thermal degradation of poly(2-methacrylamidopyridine)
source_type: primary synthesis and polymer characterization study
notes: Supports exact monomer and polymerization entry only; no BPA adsorption or water binding was tested

claim_text: MAPy was copolymerized with styrene over reported 6-40 mol percent feeds and its donor-acceptor self-association was used to form supramolecular polystyrene networks
source_title: Polystyrene supramolecular networks based on DA-AD hydrogen bonds
source_type: primary polymer synthesis and association study
notes: Supports copolymer compatibility but flags self-association that may block BPA recognition; not adsorption evidence

claim_text: N-Phenylmethacrylamide has established radical polymerization behavior and retains the aromatic amide while replacing the pyridine nitrogen with CH
source_title: Radical polymerization of N-substituted methacrylamides
notes: Near-isomass pyridine-acceptor deletion control; aqueous BPA interaction remains untested

claim_text: Linear and cross-linked polymers were prepared by free-radical polymerization of N-methacryloyl-3-aminopyridine before downstream quaternization
source_title: Anthracene labeled poly(pyridine methacrylamide) as a polymer-based chemosensor for detection of pyrophosphate
notes: Exact positional control with the same formula as P1; downstream quaternized sensing is irrelevant to BPA

claim_text: N-Methyl-N-(2-pyridyl)acrylamide is an identified C9H10N2O compound with CAS 1156926-11-2 and no amide NH donor
source_title: commercial identity record
notes: Useful soluble donor-deletion control; no performance or matched polymer-reactivity claim

claim_text: 2-Acrylamidopyridine is a double-hydrogen-bonding self-associating motif with a reported association constant about 13.4 M-1 in chloroform
source_title: A review of shape memory polymers bearing reversible binding groups
journal_or_authority: Journal of Polymer Science Part B
notes: Shows self-association must be modeled; chloroform behavior cannot be transferred to water or to BPA binding

claim_text: A 2-acrylamidopyridine/HEMA hydrogel adsorbed phenol 2,6-dimethylphenol and 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol at about 35.1 30.1 and 17.2 mg/g respectively for the reported 50 percent AP composition
source_title: Removal of phenolic compounds using HEMA/acrylamidopyridine hydrogel prepared by gamma radiation
notes: Directly supports aqueous phenol uptake but the decreasing capacity with steric bulk and absence of BPA analog tests do not support BPA selectivity

claim_text: 2-Acetamidopyridine binds a carboxylic acid through the established amidopyridine motif in dichloromethane with reported Ka about 1440 M-1
source_title: Inversion of product selectivity in an enzyme-inspired metallosupramolecular tweezer catalyzed epoxidation reaction
notes: The two-oxygen carboxylic acid synthon is not evidence that a one-oxygen phenol forms the same two-point geometry in water

claim_text: In D2O beta-CD and BPA formed a 1:1 inclusion complex and nonlinear fits of internal H3 and H5 shifts gave Ka values of 4.10e3 and 3.62e3 M-1
source_title: Determination of binding constant of molecular complex between beta-cyclodextrin and bisphenol A by using 1H NMR spectroscopy
notes: Strong direct aqueous receptor evidence; does not establish selectivity over bisphenol analogs in secondary effluent

claim_text: ROESY and crystallography support BPA inclusion in beta-CD; the solid complex is a head-to-head beta-CD dimer containing two BPA molecules
source_title: Inclusion complexes of bisphenol A with cyclomaltoheptaose beta-cyclodextrin: solubilization and structure
applicability_boundary: beta-CD BPA solution and solid complex
notes: The 2:2 solid complex remains one BPA per beta-CD and does not prove a unique aqueous pose for a fixed added rim site

claim_text: A porous polymer carrying 0.239 mmol beta-CD/g had a site-derived theoretical BPA capacity of 54.5 mg/g and a Langmuir maximum of 55.6 mg/g at 293 K
source_title: Preparation of regenerable polymeric adsorbents functionalized with beta-cyclodextrin moieties for selective recovery of bisphenol A
locator: full text and elemental-analysis site accounting
applicability_boundary: porous styrene DVB GMA particles functionalized with beta-CD
notes: Supports total-material site accounting and regeneration precedent; the low beta-CD loading also demonstrates carrier mass penalty and is not proof of BPA selectivity in municipal effluent

claim_text: A self-supporting beta-CD polymer crosslinked with 4,4-difluorodiphenylsulfone reached a maximal BPA capacity of 113 mg/g removed 90 percent of equilibrium uptake within about one minute and retained adsorption over five regeneration cycles
source_title: A crosslinked beta-cyclodextrin polymer used for rapid removal of a broad-spectrum of organic micropollutants from water
source_type: primary synthesis dynamic adsorption and regeneration study
notes: Strong engineering positive control but broad-spectrum micropollutant removal is not BPA-specific selectivity and the route is established prior art

claim_text: A flexible-rigid double-crosslinked beta-CD polymer made in aqueous phase showed rapid uptake of BPA 3-phenylphenol and ethinyl estradiol and reported tolerance to pH ionic strength and humic acid with methanol regeneration
source_title: Simple synthesis of a swellable porous beta-cyclodextrin-based polymer in the aqueous phase for the rapid removal of organic micro-pollutants from water
source_type: primary synthesis adsorption and matrix study
notes: High-overlap practical-water benchmark from Nanjing University; not evidence of BPA-specific competitive selectivity or a new biomimetic mechanism

claim_text: Water-insoluble beta-CD polymers crosslinked by BTCA adsorbed BPA most strongly at low BTCA content corresponding to high beta-CD content per unit polymer mass
source_title: Polymerization of beta-cyclodextrin with 1,2,3,4-butanetetracarboxylic dianhydride: synthesis structural characterization and bisphenol A adsorption capacity
notes: Supports the inactive-mass rule and high-site-fraction architecture; does not supply a novel selective mechanism

claim_text: An aqueous rigid-flexible double-crosslinked beta-CD polymer had BPA qmax 71.30 mg/g reached equilibrium within 30 minutes retained 94.7 percent saturated capacity after five cycles and was tested in plastic-factory wastewater
source_title: A double-crosslinked cyclodextrin-based porous polymer for effective removal of bisphenol A: preparation adsorption behavior and mechanism
source_type: primary synthesis adsorption regeneration and wastewater study
notes: Practical engineering precedent further crowds BPA-2E novelty; industrial wastewater is not municipal secondary effluent and competitive bisphenol selectivity remains open

claim_text: A sevenfold primary-rim styrene-functionalized beta-CD monomer was copolymerized with a cationic methacrylate into permanently porous networks and tested across PFCA PFSA and salt matrices
source_title: A tunable porous beta-cyclodextrin polymer platform to understand and improve anionic PFAS removal
source_type: primary synthesis characterization and adsorption study
notes: This directly implements the core PFBS-1 architecture and makes formulation tuning crowded prior art rather than a new candidate

claim_text: At 1 mg/L adsorbent and 1 ug/L each PFAS the cationic beta-CD polymer removed about 94 percent PFBS in nanopure water but only about 38 percent in 1 mM sodium sulfate while PFOS retained about 79 percent removal
source_title: A tunable porous beta-cyclodextrin polymer platform to understand and improve anionic PFAS removal
applicability_boundary: low-concentration batch PFAS mixture after 48 hours
notes: Shows short-chain PFBS capture is strongly salt-limited and does not establish preferential PFBS capture over long-chain PFAS in secondary effluent

claim_text: Positive amine functionality on the beta-CD perimeter increased short-chain PFAS complexation relative to neutral beta-CD by up to about 20-fold depending on the guest
source_title: Enhanced host-guest complexation of short chain perfluoroalkyl substances with positively charged beta-cyclodextrin derivatives
journal_or_authority: Journal of Inclusion Phenomena and Macrocyclic Chemistry
applicability_boundary: soluble charged beta-CD derivatives and short-chain PFAS
notes: Supports same-host electrostatic plus cavity design but does not prove PFBS-specific preference or salt-resistant polymer adsorption

claim_text: A guanidinium receptor at an aqueous interface bound common oxoanions in the order sulfate above dihydrogen phosphate above nitrate above nitrite with weak halide interactions
source_title: Molecular recognition and hydration energy mismatch combine to inform ion binding selectivity at aqueous interfaces
notes: Warns that PFBS headgroup receptors may preferentially bind sulfate and phosphate; interfacial ordering is not a soluble or bulk-polymer constant

claim_text: A rigid bis-2-pyridyl guanidinium receptor selectively bound sulfate in 10 percent water 90 percent methanol with stepwise log K values about 3.78 and 2.10
source_title: A simple guanidinium motif for the selective binding and extraction of sulfate
source_type: primary solution binding extraction and computation study
applicability_boundary: sulfate in aqueous methanol and liquid-liquid extraction
notes: Directly demonstrates sulfate competition for rigid guanidinium geometry but not binding in pure water or to PFBS

claim_text: Guanidinocalix5arene used multiple guanidinium groups and a hydrophobic macrocyclic region to bind PFOA and PFOS strongly in water
source_title: Guanidinocalix5arene for sensitive fluorescence detection and magnetic removal of perfluorinated pollutants
source_type: primary aqueous recognition and removal study
applicability_boundary: PFOA and PFOS with high-mass amphiphilic macrocycle
notes: Supports dual-zone PFAS recognition in water but not a low-mass C4 PFBS slot; magnetic iron oxide implementation is outside the preferred mass-efficient route

claim_text: A synthetic receptor recognized C2-C5 perfluoroalkyl carboxylates with micromolar affinity in acetonitrile and extracted them from buffered water into an organic phase in the presence of inorganic anions
source_title: Selective recognition and extraction of short-chain perfluoroalkyl carboxylates by a supramolecular receptor
source_type: primary crystallography solution binding and extraction study
notes: Shows short-chain molecular recognition is possible but headgroup solvent and process do not transfer to PFBS aqueous adsorption

claim_text: PDB 1JZZ is a 3.80 A D. radiodurans 50S-roxithromycin direct structure; the visible part of the ROX etheroxime extension does not contact 23S rRNA or ribosomal protein while the ROX core does bind 23S rRNA
source_title: Structural basis for the interaction of antibiotics with the peptidyl transferase centre in eubacteria
notes: Supports a shared macrolide binding pose but not a complete ROX-specific side-chain geometry

claim_text: Higher-quality erythromycin and azithromycin ribosome structures differed from early D. radiodurans models and attributed the discrepancy to density quality or interpretation rather than species specificity
source_title: Revisiting the structures of several antibiotics bound to the bacterial ribosome
notes: ROX was not remeasured; the study lowers confidence in atom-level use of 1JZZ but does not directly invalidate every ROX coordinate

claim_text: In one E. coli ribosomal complex the overall dissociation constants were 36 nM for erythromycin 20 nM for roxithromycin and 8 nM for clarithromycin
source_title: Erythromycin Roxithromycin and Clarithromycin Use of Slow-Binding Kinetics to Compare Their In Vitro Interaction with a Bacterial Ribosomal Complex Active in Peptide Bond Formation
notes: The natural binding system does not show ROX preference over clarithromycin and the kinetics do not define a material slit

claim_text: DFT models for PAF-6 and macrolides gave adsorption energies of -12.13 kcal/mol for ROX -16.21 for clarithromycin and -23.47 for azithromycin
source_title: Determination of Six Macrolide Antibiotics in Chicken Sample by Liquid Chromatography-Tandem Mass Spectrometry Based on Solid Phase Extraction of Porous Aromatic Framework
journal_or_authority: Journal of Analytical Methods in Chemistry
applicability_boundary: PAF-6 macrolide model and organic-solvent SPE
notes: The model predicts weaker ROX adsorption and does not provide a strict experimentally resolved pore-bound geometry

claim_text: Thermodynamic pKa values measured by capillary electrophoresis were 8.82 for ROX 8.83 for erythromycin 8.79 for clarithromycin and 8.96 for azithromycin
source_title: Determination of thermodynamic pKa values of pharmaceuticals from five different groups using capillary electrophoresis
notes: Acidic material sites will encounter four similarly protonated macrolides near neutral pH and cannot by charge alone provide ROX selectivity

claim_text: A roxithromycin dummy-template molecularly imprinted monolithic extraction column showed class-specific extraction of multiple macrolide antibiotics
source_title: Preparation and Application of Molecularly Imprinted Monolithic Extraction Column for the Selective Microextraction of Multiple Macrolide Antibiotics from Animal Muscles
notes: Direct evidence that even ROX-derived imprinting may produce macrolide-class recognition rather than ROX single-analyte specificity

claim_text: Across five wastewater treatment plants the mean ROX effluent concentrations were 197 241 200 199 and 254 ng/L
source_title: Fate of Macrolide Antibiotics with Different Wastewater Treatment Technologies
notes: The 197-254 ng/L range is across plant means not all individual samples and shows why mg/L adsorption tests cannot replace trace competitive validation

claim_text: A 4+2 imine cage directly included clarithromycin in CDCl3 and showed optical differentiation from selected macrolides in chloroform or chloroform-methanol
source_title: Nanoarchitectonics of a covalent organic supramolecular cage for fluorescent visual detection of macrolides
notes: No ROX or erythromycin comparison and no pure-water binding; it cannot freeze a water-compatible ROX slit

claim_text: The ERRgamma-BPA structure contains polar contacts at both phenolic ends but structure-activity evidence shows one phenolic hydroxyl is sufficient for near-BPA activity
source_title: Direct evidence revealing structural elements essential for the high binding ability of bisphenol A to human estrogen-related receptor-gamma
notes: Supports a primary polar anchor plus hydrophobic environment; does not support two-ended anchoring as necessary

claim_text: Water-soluble sulfonated calix6arene binds BPA but free-host constants favor BPB over BPA and distinguish BPA from BPF only modestly
source_title: Complexation of bisphenol A with calix[6]arene-polymer conjugates
notes: Not calix4; does not establish BPA-specific two-ended binding

claim_text: An immobilized sulfonated calix6arene surface gave indirect competition constants for BPA BPF BPS and BPB in phosphate buffer with 10 percent methanol
source_title: Inclusion of Bisphenols by a Self-Assembled Monolayer of Thiolated Calix[6]arene on a Gold Surface
locator: full text p5417 Figure 4 and Table 1
notes: Indirect surface assay and mixed solvent; no independent two-ended geometry proof

claim_text: Porous CalP4 directly removed BPA from water with reported qmax 403 mg/g total material and rapid uptake and could be regenerated
source_title: Porous Polycalix[4]arenes for Fast and Efficient Removal of Organic Micropollutants from Water
notes: Strong direct prior art; lacks BPA analog competition DOM secondary effluent and matched cavity deletion controls

claim_text: Alkenyl calix4arene was copolymerized into a porous capillary monolith for preconcentration of multiple bisphenols
source_title: Preconcentration of bisphenols by using calix[4]arene derivatives modified porous polymer monolith coupled with HPLC
notes: Supports polymerizable immobilization but not water-treatment capacity or BPA over BPF BPS selectivity

claim_text: A nonmacrocyclic hyper-cross-linked polymer reported BPA qmax 562 mg/g and 99.1 percent removal efficiency after the fifth cycle
source_title: A nitrogen-rich hyper-cross-linked polymer for bisphenol A adsorption
notes: High capacity and regeneration cannot establish calixarene necessity

claim_text: Dissolved effluent organic matter showed measurable association with BPA and altered its apparent environmental behavior
source_title: Interaction of Bisphenol A with Dissolved Organic Matter from Municipal Wastewater Treatment Plant Effluents
notes: Supports treating free BPA and DOM association as a mechanism variable; does not quantify competition for the proposed calixarene material

claim_text: Neutron reflectometry showed PFBS PFHxA PFHxS PFNA PFOS and FOSA incorporate into a phospholipid bilayer with interaction depending on chain length and headgroup; all but PFNA were removed by the reported gentle water rinse
source_title: Interactions of perfluoroalkyl substances with a phospholipid bilayer studied by neutron reflectometry
journal_or_authority: Journal of Colloid and Interface Science
notes: Supports actual membrane incorporation and chain/headgroup dependence; does not prove PFOA or PFNA selective adsorption or an engineered regeneration cycle

claim_text: PFAS partitioned into model phospholipid membranes and bacteria with incorporation increasing with fluorinated chain length and being greater for sulfonates than carboxylates
source_title: Partitioning and Accumulation of Perfluoroalkyl Substances in Model Lipid Bilayers and Bacteria
source_type: primary membrane and bacterial partition study
notes: Supports a real biological accumulation reference but primarily a monotonic class trend rather than target-specific selection

claim_text: PFOA at submicromolar to reported 0.1 nM conditions altered DPPC bilayer transition enthalpy vesicle size distribution and secondary-solute partitioning
source_title: Nanomolar PFOA Concentrations Affect Lipid Membrane Structure: Consequences for Bioconcentration Mechanisms
notes: Supports low-concentration membrane interaction; does not define an artificial recognition unit or PFOA analog selectivity

claim_text: At 1 ug/L tests significant sorption occurred for eight positively charged micropollutants including erythromycin clarithromycin and roxithromycin; equilibrium partition and effective diffusion varied with biofilm thickness
source_title: Diffusion and sorption of organic micropollutants in biofilms with varying thicknesses
source_type: primary biofilm diffusion and sorption study
notes: Supports actual biofilm sorption and thickness-dependent transport but indicates cationic-class rather than ROX-specific recognition

claim_text: Aerobic granular sludge achieved high ROX removal in the tested low-CN reactor; batch tests attributed ROX removal partly to AGS adsorption and humic-acid-like EPS increased under the same condition
source_title: Enhancement of PPCPs removal by shaped microbial community of aerobic granular sludge under condition of low C/N ratio influent
applicability_boundary: aerobic granular sludge ROX and SMZ
notes: Supports actual biological-material adsorption of ROX; co-variation of humic-like EPS is not proof of a unique causal site or ROX selectivity

claim_text: Four PFBS-tolerant strains removed about 29 to 38 percent PFBS at 100 ug/L over four days and spectroscopy implicated protein and polysaccharide EPS binding sites
source_title: Composition and Morphological Characteristics of Extracellular Polymeric Substances of Different Tolerant Bacteria Under Perfluorobutanesulfonic Acid PFBS Stress
applicability_boundary: tolerant bacterial EPS under PFBS stress
notes: Supports actual EPS-associated PFBS biosorption but not secondary-effluent trace selectivity sulfate tolerance or a translatable site geometry

claim_text: Radiolabeled ROX reached intracellular to extracellular concentration ratios of 14 to 190 in phagocytic cells and about half of recovered drug in macrophages was lysosome associated
source_title: Cellular uptake and subcellular distribution of roxithromycin and erythromycin in phagocytic cells
applicability_boundary: human and animal macrophages and neutrophils
notes: Direct ROX accumulation prototype; uptake was reversible and inhibited by acidic external pH but the study does not prove ROX specificity or isolate pure ion trapping

claim_text: After oral ROX administration human alveolar-cell concentrations were about twice plasma and ten times epithelial-lining-fluid concentrations
source_title: Pulmonary disposition of roxithromycin
applicability_boundary: human bronchoalveolar lavage after therapeutic dosing
notes: Supports in-vivo cellular enrichment but not a wastewater adsorption rate or selective material mechanism

claim_text: ROX directly interacted with phosphatidylinositol-containing liposomes and modeled orientation placed sugar regions in the hydrophobic domain with the macrocycle near the interface
source_title: Interactions of macrolide antibiotics with phospholipids computer-aided conformational analysis and studies on acellular and cell culture models
notes: Supports a nonprotein membrane-partition prototype but the reported order ERY no greater than ROX below erythromycylamine and AZI contradicts ROX specificity

claim_text: ROX ERY and CLA have closely similar principal basic pKa values so ideal acidic-compartment ion trapping largely cancels pKa differences while dibasic AZI may be favored
source_title: Quantitative Modeling of Selective Lysosomal Targeting for Drug Design
notes: Mechanism support combined with E066; not a direct ROX material experiment and not proof of a specific AZI to ROX ratio

claim_text: A ROX-imprinted composite membrane in ethanol reported 2.24 umol/g binding and ROX selectivity factors of 1.75 versus erythromycin 2.46 versus azithromycin and 2.67 versus erythromycin ethylsuccinate with facilitated permeation proposed
source_title: Synthesis and Properties of Molecularly Imprinted Composite Membranes of Roxithromycin
applicability_boundary: polysulfone-supported imprinted composite membrane in ethanol
notes: Direct selective-transport precedent; does not establish neutral-water secondary-effluent performance or coupling to an acidic receiving phase

claim_text: In Chlorella pyrenoidosa cultivation ROX biosorption and bioaccumulation were minor while later biodegradation contributed more strongly
source_title: Interactive effects of roxithromycin and freshwater microalgae Toxicity and removal mechanism
applicability_boundary: 0.1 to 2 mg/L ROX and 21-day algae cultivation
notes: Negative evidence against using algal cell-wall adsorption as a high-efficiency biomimetic prototype

claim_text: Reconstituted MacAB-TolC experiments directly monitored ATP-dependent ROX transport into liposomes
source_title: Quantitative real-time analysis of the efflux by the MacAB-TolC tripartite efflux pump clarifies the role of ATP hydrolysis within mechanotransmission mechanism
applicability_boundary: reconstituted protein pump at micromolar ROX
notes: Confirms real active ROX transport but MacAB is macrolide-polyspecific protein machinery and is not a direct nonprotein adsorbent template
